pi_items.source: Task specification; 24" cover diameter (radius 12) is the most common standard manhole cover size per search of manufacturer specifications
Task specification; verified as most common standard manhole cover size (24" diameter) per ASTM and manufacturer specifications
Task specification; pick holes are real features on manhole covers used for lifting lugs, with hook points typically 0.5" wide, making 1.5" diameter holes plausible
Code analysis of verifier.py: Frame range 14.0-16.0 (±1), Cover range 11.0-13.0 (±1), Pick Hole range 0.5-1.0 (±0.25)
Task description specifies full text 'MANHOLE FRAME & COVER' above and 'SCALE: NTS' below; metadata requires only 'MANHOLE' substring
ANSI31 confirmed as standard hatch pattern for iron/masonry sections per ASME Y14.3 standards, available by default in LibreCAD
Task specification; reasonable extents given frame radius of 15 inches, allowing 3-inch margin on all sides
Task description explicitly states units as Inches
Downloaded and verified: 1.1MB file from jscad sample-files repository (Apache-licensed)
Code verification: import ezdxf successful, file parsing tested on floorplan.dxf

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task requiring an AI agent to create a technical drawing of a manhole frame and cover in LibreCAD. The task specifies geometric dimensions with a frame radius of 15 inches (30-inch diameter), cover radius of 12 inches (24-inch diameter—standard manhole cover size), and two pick holes with radius 0.75 inches (1.5-inch diameter—plausible for lifting mechanisms). The drawing requires ANSI31 hatch pattern (a real, standardized pattern for technical drawings), specified layers, and text annotations. Critical finding: The metadata claims a uniform tolerance of 1.0, but the verifier.py code actually applies ±1.0 tolerance to frame and cover radii, but only ±0.25 tolerance to pick hole radius. The supporting data includes a real 1.1MB DXF file (floorplan.dxf) downloaded from the jscad sample-files public repository, and ezdxf 1.4.3 is available for verification.
